title: CHAP Task / Artefact / Override
description: Schemas for the Task, Artefact, and Override primitives.

kind.description: Workspace-defined task kind (e.g. draft_response, classify, code_review).

rule.description: Decision rule. One of: any_one_approves, all_approve, quorum:<n>, weighted_vote:<threshold>, weighted_vote_with_veto:<threshold>.

routing_hints.description: Optional routing signals for the task. CHAP defines the field shape; semantics (thresholds, calibration, taxonomies) are operator-defined. Profile routing/1.0 defines methods that consume these hints. Fields are signed into the evidence envelope hash and queryable from the audit log.

deadline.description: When the work is needed by. Distinct from review.deadline which is when a review must be returned.

max_cost_usd.description: Budget ceiling for fulfilling the task. Operator-defined currency convention; USD by default.

risk_tier.description: Org-specific risk classification (e.g. 'pci-cardholder', 'gdpr-special', 'internal-only'). Opaque to protocol; carried for audit and routing.

supersedes.description: If set, the id of the task this one supersedes.

parent.description: Optional parent task id for sub-tasks.

kind.description: Standard kinds: draft, decision, override, abstention, escalation, citation_set, snapshot, capture_fragment. Implementations may add kinds with a `schema` reference.

schema.description: URI of the JSON Schema for this artefact's content. Required for non-standard kinds.

based_on.description: For override and decision artefacts, the artefact id this one refers to.

logical_id.description: Optional. Stable identifier for the thing the artefact is about — durable across revisions. Two artefacts that share a logical_id are two versions of the same underlying item. Producers SHOULD assign on first creation and reuse on every revision, override or supersession. CHAP itself reads only `id`; logical_id is for higher-layer version-graph projection.

instance_id.description: Optional. Stable identifier for this particular version. If present, MUST equal the artefact's content_hash or be deterministically derived from it, so consumers can detect byte-identical revisions across the chain. Implementations that do not need a separate instance handle MAY omit this or set it equal to `id`.

content.description: Inline content. Implementations MAY substitute a URI reference for large content.

content_uri.description: If content is too large to inline, a URI to where it lives. The content_hash MUST still cover the canonical content bytes.

content_hash.description: SHA-256 of the canonical content bytes (JCS for JSON content).

confidence.description: DEPRECATED in favour of routing_hints.confidence. Kept for backward compatibility within v0.x. Agents only; model-specific calibration applies.

routing_hints.description: Optional measurement signals from production. CHAP defines the field shape; semantics (calibration, cost models) are operator-defined. Profile routing/1.0 defines methods that consume these hints. Fields are signed into the evidence envelope hash.

confidence.description: Model's self-reported confidence in the artefact. Model-specific calibration; include model_id for consumers to interpret. CHAP makes no claim about cross-model comparability.

model_id.description: Identifier of the model or composite that produced the artefact. Used by consumers to look up calibration data. Recommended whenever confidence is present.

cost_consumed_usd.description: Actual cost incurred to produce this artefact. Operator-defined currency convention; USD by default.

latency_ms.description: Wall-clock time to produce the artefact, in milliseconds.

OverrideContent.description: Content payload for an artefact of kind=override.

diff.description: JSON Patch operations (RFC 6902) with optional `from` on replace for context.

intent_preserved.description: Optional. If the based_on artefact carries a logical_id, set to true when the override refines the expression of the underlying intent (same decision, better delivery) and false when the override substitutes a different decision. Informational; CHAP does not constrain semantics. The override SHOULD carry the same logical_id as its based_on target.

logical_id.description: Optional. SHOULD equal the logical_id of the based_on artefact when that artefact carries one. Lets consumers project overrides onto a version graph keyed by logical_id.
